meta.title: Unterm — AI 可控制的終端
meta.desc: 面向 agent 控制的跨平台終端：透過 MCP、HTTP 和 CLI 驅動 pane、cwd、截圖與工作階段。
hero.badge: v0.12.2 — 已簽署、已公證，適用於 macOS / Linux / Windows
hero.subtitle: 透過 MCP、HTTP 和 CLI 把 pane、cwd、截圖與工作階段狀態暴露給 Claude Code、Cursor，或你自己的 agent，讓它直接接管整個終端。
hero.cta.download: 下載適合你裝置的版本
hero.meta: 面向 agent 的終端 · 本地優先 · 無雲端 · 無登入
tagline.title: AI 控制終端，MCP 負責落地。
tagline.body: 大多數終端把 AI 嵌進二進位裡。Unterm 反著來：把 AI 放在外面，讓任何外部 agent 都能透過 MCP 抓握終端這個表面。被控制的是終端，真正執行的是 agent。
persona.title: Three ways to use Unterm
persona.subtitle: We didn't build for one workflow. The MCP surface means each persona gets the same control plane through different entry points.
persona.ai.title: AI engineers
persona.ai.body: Drive multiple terminal panes from Claude Code, Cursor, or your own agent. Each pane is one task; your agent picks where to type. Multi-instance NATO names (alpha, bravo, charlie…) make routing across windows trivial.
persona.ai.bullets.1: Director / worker pattern: outer agent supervises inner agent in a pane
persona.ai.bullets.2: Long-running watcher: kick off a build, poll until idle, decide next step
persona.ai.bullets.3: Multi-pane orchestration: fan work across projects, aggregate results
persona.ai.bullets.4: Recording with token redaction for review and fine-tune
persona.ops.title: DevOps & SREs
persona.ops.body: Cron-friendly CLI for everything you can do in the GUI. unterm-cli ships in every release; pipe --json through anywhere downstream that wants raw JSON-RPC. Same surface for ops scripts, dashboards, runbooks.
persona.ops.bullets.1: Auto proxy detection: macOS scutil / Windows registry / GNOME gsettings
persona.ops.bullets.2: Headless screenshots and pane reads for incident docs
persona.ops.bullets.3: Recording → markdown for runbook generation, PII-redacted by default
persona.ops.bullets.4: No telemetry, no login, every server bound to 127.0.0.1
persona.power.title: Power users & OSS hackers
persona.power.body: MIT-licensed WezTerm fork with first-class agent integration. Patches accepted on GitHub. Build it yourself; the binary CLI ships in every release. Web Settings UI is a Tailwind + Alpine SPA — fork the page, change the colors.
persona.power.bullets.1: Universal arm64 + x86_64 macOS binary, signed with Developer ID
persona.power.bullets.4: 9 locales out of the box, system locale auto-detect
install.title: 按平台下載
install.subtitle: 選擇適合你系統的已簽署安裝包，下面的直鏈會直接跳到 GitHub Releases。
install.unix_detail: Detects OS + arch, downloads the right artifact for the latest release. macOS gets the signed + notarized DMG into /Applications. Linux uses apt when available, falls back to the AppImage in ~/.local/bin.
install.win_detail: Pulls the latest Unterm-x.y.z-x64.msi from GitHub Releases and runs msiexec with a basic UI. UAC will prompt for elevation.
install.macos.title: macOS
install.linux.title: Linux
install.windows.title: Windows
features.title: Four control surfaces, one engine
features.subtitle: Every Unterm window starts a local MCP server and an HTTP settings server. Read and write the same JSON state from any of them.
features.mcp.title: MCP server
features.mcp.body: Line-delimited JSON-RPC on 127.0.0.1:19876, auth-token gated. Spawn shells, read pane state, capture screenshots, control sessions.
features.cli.title: unterm-cli
features.cli.body: Same surface from any shell, cron job, or script. Thin JSON-RPC client over the local MCP — no duplicated business logic.
features.web.title: Web Settings
features.web.body: Modern config UI in the browser, not the cell grid. Tailwind + Alpine SPA at 127.0.0.1:19877. Themes, proxy, recordings, language.
features.i18n.title: 9 languages
features.i18n.body: en / 简体 / 繁體 / 日本語 / 한국어 / Deutsch / Français / Italiano / हिन्दी out of the box. System locale auto-detect.
features.rec.title: Session recording
features.rec.body: OSC 133 block-segmented markdown with built-in redaction. Recordings live in <cwd>/.unterm/sessions/.
features.shot.title: Region screenshots
features.shot.body: One-click region capture from the status bar. PNG to disk, image to clipboard, path to text clipboard.
features.proxy.title: Auto proxy detect
features.proxy.body: Reads macOS scutil / Windows registry / GNOME gsettings / env. One-toggle on/off. No URL config.
features.engine.title: GPU rendering
features.engine.body: Built on a customized WezTerm engine — Metal on macOS, OpenGL on Linux, DirectX via ANGLE on Windows.
whatsnew.title: What's new in v0.12
whatsnew.subtitle: Three weeks of Windows polish + the multi-instance foundation that AI agents need to drive a fleet of terminals.
whatsnew.flash.title: White-flash fix
whatsnew.flash.body: Windows used to flash bright white between window create and the first GL frame. Root cause: WindowProc intercepted WM_ERASEBKGND but never painted, so the DWM redirection bitmap stayed in its uninitialized white state. Now we manually FillRect the client area with BLACK_BRUSH before returning.
whatsnew.instances.title: Multi-instance NATO names
whatsnew.instances.body: Each Unterm process owns one instance with a NATO-phonetic name (alpha, bravo, charlie…). MCP instance.list enumerates all live instances; agents pick by cwd, title, or start order. Window title becomes Unterm — alpha — Project for visual mapping.
whatsnew.utf8.title: Auto UTF-8 on Windows
whatsnew.utf8.body: PowerShell and cmd.exe spawned by Unterm get UTF-8 output by default — no more mojibake on zh-CN, ja-JP, ko-KR Windows installs. Agents don't have to fix the user's $PROFILE; we wrap at spawn time with chcp 65001 + Console.OutputEncoding setup.
whatsnew.cwd.title: CWD in the status bar
whatsnew.cwd.body: Active pane's working directory shows up between the shell name and dimensions, with $HOME → ~ and middle truncation. Click to copy the full path. Works across local panes, mux clients, and SSH-forwarded sessions.
principles.title: Five principles, no exceptions
principles.local.title: Local-first, no cloud
principles.local.body: Every server, every API endpoint, every recording lives on 127.0.0.1. No login, no telemetry, no subscription. Your shell history is yours.
principles.outside.title: AI lives outside the terminal
principles.outside.body: No chat overlay, no ghost-text autocomplete, no inline AI panel. The terminal is the surface — Claude Code, Cursor, your scripts grip it through MCP.
principles.mcp.title: MCP is a first-class surface
principles.mcp.body: Every product feature ships with an MCP method and a CLI subcommand on day one. If it can't be driven from outside, it doesn't ship.
principles.parity.title: Cross-platform parity is correctness
principles.parity.body: A feature that works on Windows but bails on macOS or Linux is a bug, not a 'not yet supported.' Mac, Linux, Windows ship together.
principles.subtraction.title: Subtraction over decoration
principles.subtraction.body: When a feature is awkward, delete it instead of redesigning it. No right-click menu, no Cmd+Q confirmation, no manual proxy URL config — they used to exist, then we cut them and the product got better.
cmp.title: How Unterm differs
cmp.subtitle: Three terminals reset the bar in 2026. They each picked a different lane.
cmp.note: Comparison reflects publicly documented features as of 2026-05-01. Other terminals may have closed-source or roadmap items not listed.
dl.title: Get Unterm 0.12.2
dl.subtitle: macOS bundle is signed with a Developer ID and Apple-notarized. Linux .deb / AppImage and Windows .msi / .zip are also published.
faq.title: Frequently asked
faq.what.a: A cross-platform terminal emulator with built-in MCP, HTTP, and CLI control surfaces. The product thesis: terminal as MCP-controllable surface, so any external AI agent can drive it from outside instead of having an AI baked into the terminal itself.
faq.mcp.q: How does it work with Claude Code, Cursor, or other agents?
faq.mcp.a: Each Unterm window starts a local MCP server (TCP, JSON-RPC, auth-token gated). Point your MCP client at 127.0.0.1:<port> — the port + token are written to ~/.unterm/server.json on launch. The agent can spawn shells, run commands, read pane state, capture screenshots, toggle recording, and switch settings.
faq.multi.q: How do I drive multiple Unterm windows from one agent?
faq.multi.a: Each Unterm process is one instance with a NATO-phonetic name (alpha, bravo, …) recorded in ~/.unterm/instances/<name>.json. Call instance.list on any one of them to enumerate; pick by cwd / title / start order; connect to that instance's port with its auth token. ~/.unterm/active.json points at the most recent live instance for single-instance fallbacks.
faq.warp.q: How is this different from Warp?
faq.warp.a: Warp embeds AI inside a closed cloud orchestrator (Oz) — external tools like Claude Code can't drive Warp from outside. Unterm picks the third lane: keep AI out of the terminal, expose the terminal itself as an MCP-controllable surface, and let any agent grip it. No cloud, no login.
faq.privacy.a: Nowhere external. MCP and Web Settings servers bind to 127.0.0.1 only. Session recordings land under <project>/.unterm/sessions/ with built-in redaction for tokens. There is no telemetry, no analytics, no login, no cloud round-trip. Your shell history is yours.
faq.script.a: Use unterm-cli: session list, proxy status, theme set midnight, session record start, screenshot. Pass --json to any subcommand for raw JSON-RPC output suitable for shell pipelines and cron jobs.
faq.platform.q: Which platforms ship signed and notarized?
faq.platform.a: macOS: universal arm64 + x86_64 DMG signed with a Developer ID and Apple-notarized + stapled (no Gatekeeper warnings). Linux: .deb for Debian/Ubuntu and AppImage for any distro. Windows: WiX-built MSI installer and portable .zip. All published to GitHub Releases on every minor tag.
faq.opensrc.a: Yes — MIT licensed. Built on top of the WezTerm engine for renderer / font / SSH / mux work, with a thin Unterm product layer (MCP, Web Settings, recording, i18n, signing pipeline) on top. Source at github.com/unzooai/unterm.
faq.contrib.a: Open an issue or PR on GitHub. The project tracks bugs, feature requests, and discussion in one queue. We bundle accumulated fixes into minor releases — patch-level versions don't trigger CI builds. Documentation lives in-repo at /docs.
community.title: Community & contact
community.subtitle: Open queue, no gatekeeping. The right place for your question depends on what kind of question it is.
community.github.title: GitHub Issues
community.github.body: Bug reports, feature requests, regressions. The triage queue is single — every report gets read.
community.discussions.title: GitHub Discussions
community.discussions.body: Questions about MCP integration patterns, agent setup, workflow design. Other users may have hit the same thing.
community.email.title: Email the author
community.email.body: Direct line for security issues, partnerships, or anything that doesn't fit a public forum.
sister.title: Sister projects
sister.subtitle: Same author, same MCP-first design philosophy, different domains.
sister.solomd: Solo, AI-driven Markdown editor with offline RAG, git rollback, and the same MCP control plane. solomd.app.
sister.unflick: Video player for humans and AI: GUI, CLI, and an MCP server exposing playback, transcription, and library tools. unflick.app.
footer.stack: Made with Rust · WezTerm engine · MCP · Tailwind + Alpine
